title: Macrosamanea prancei
taxonomy: Macrosamanea prancei belongs to the family Caesalpiniaceae, which is a group of flowering plants. It is a part of the genus Macrosamanea and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Macrosamanea prancei is classified as a tree species and is known for its distinctive characteristics and growth habits. This plant is closely related to other species in the Caesalpiniaceae family, such as Samanea and Libidibia. Further classification may include its order, family, and kingdom, which would place it in the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Macrosamanea prancei, also known as the prancei samanea, is a medicinal plant species within the Caesalpiniaceae family, primarily found in South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Macrosamanea prancei include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Macrosamanea prancei is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it may also be used to make herbal tinctures and extracts, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions.
